# agent-help implementation brief for LLM agents

Use this brief when a user asks you to make a CLI agent-native, implement agent-help, add --agent-help, add --agent-out, or make command output easier for AI agents to consume.

Goal: implement agent-native CLI surfaces without breaking human or software output.

Audience split:
- humans: default output and --help
- software: --json
- agents: --agent-help and --agent-out

Do not replace --json. agent-help is for agents, not software integrations.

Two layers:
- AHF (Agent Help Format): defines --agent-help output and the ok/err protocol envelope for --agent-out
- TOON: defines --agent-out result bodies. See https://github.com/toon-format/spec

## Core recommendation: --agent-help

Implement --agent-help for non-trivial CLIs where agents need to discover commands and construct invocations.

Flag rule:
- --agent-help is a trailing global flag.
- valid: tool --agent-help
- valid: tool subcmd --agent-help
- valid: tool group subcmd --agent-help
- invalid: tool --agent-help subcmd
- invalid: tool --agent-help group subcmd

Add this breadcrumb, or an equivalent pointer, to normal human --help output:
LLM agent? Use --agent-help for token-optimized usage.

Discovery flow:
1. Agent runs: tool --help
2. Agent sees breadcrumb.
3. Agent runs: tool --agent-help
4. Agent picks a command from AH1 index.
5. Agent runs: tool subcmd --agent-help
6. Agent reads AH2 detail and invokes command correctly.

## Recommended: --agent-out

Implement --agent-out for commands that return structured runtime results: lists, records, search results, statuses, IDs, summaries, logs, diagnostics, or anything an agent may need to inspect or act on.

Output shape:
- Line 1: AHF ok/err status line (always AHF)
- Lines 2+: TOON result body (see below)
- Final lines: AHF next/hint records as needed

Do not emit human tables, markdown tables, color, decorative headings, prose paragraphs, or JSON for --agent-out.

## AHF syntax (for --agent-help output and --agent-out envelope)

AHF is dense text for LLM agents.

Rules:
- UTF-8 plain text; ASCII preferred.
- One record per line.
- First token is record type.
- key=value for metadata.
- Quote only values containing whitespace or reserved delimiters.
- Use _ for null, unknown, or not applicable.
- Use | for short lists inside one field.
- Include exact next commands for pagination, inspection, or recommended follow-up.
- Output should remain useful if truncated after any line.

AHF record prefixes:
- ah1: agent-help index (root --agent-help)
- ah2: agent-help command detail (subcmd --agent-help)
- ok: successful runtime status line
- err: error status line
- warn: non-fatal warning (after ok, before TOON body)
- cmd: command entry in AH1
- use: canonical invocation in AH2
- arg: positional argument definition in AH2
- flag: flag definition in AH2
- ex: valid example invocation in AH2
- hint: direct correction after err
- next: exact continuation or recommended follow-up command
- more?: pointer to AH2 detail in AH1 output; not a shell command

Scalar types (used in arg and flag records):
- str: string
- int: integer
- num: number
- bool: true/false
- path: filesystem path
- url: URL
- id: opaque identifier
- ts: timestamp
- date: calendar date
- dur: duration such as 30s, 5m, 2h, 7d
- kv: key:value
- enum(a|b): one value from listed options

## --agent-help AH1 index format

Returned by: tool --agent-help

Format:
ah1 <tool> :: <purpose>
cmd <command-signature> :: <purpose>
cmd <group> <command-signature> :: <purpose>
more? <tool> <cmd> --agent-help

Rules:
- One cmd line per invocable command.
- Flatten nested commands into command paths.
- Include required args inline.
- Include only highest-value optional flags.
- No aliases, author, version, prose, examples, or detailed flag docs.
- Sort by likely agent usage if known.
- Target under 300 tokens.
- more? is a pointer record, not a shell command. The ? signals "want more information?".

Example:
ah1 mem :: project memory for agents
cmd node add <text> --type TYPE [--tag K:V...] :: store node
cmd node list [--type TYPE] [--limit int] :: list nodes
cmd search query <search> [--type TYPE] [--limit int] :: search nodes
more? mem <cmd> --agent-help

## --agent-help AH2 command detail format

Returned by: tool subcmd --agent-help

Format:
ah2 <tool> <command-path>
use <canonical invocation>
arg <name>:<type> <req|opt> :: <purpose>
flag --<name>:<type> <req|opt|repeat> [default=<value>] :: <purpose>
ex <valid example invocation>

Rules:
- Include required args and required flags.
- Include optional flags that materially change behavior.
- State defaults only when non-obvious.
- Use repeat for repeatable flags.
- Every ex should work as written; omit examples that may drift.
- Target under 150 tokens.

Example:
ah2 mem node add
use mem node add <text> --type TYPE [--tag K:V...]
arg text:str req :: node text
flag --type:enum(decision|fact|pattern|observation) req :: node type
flag --tag:kv repeat :: metadata
ex mem node add "postgres 15" --type fact --tag project:myapp

## Error hint format

Use for invalid invocations and runtime errors.

Format:
err <code> [key=value...]
hint <direct correction>
use <canonical invocation if useful>

Rules:
- Do not respond only with run --help.
- For enum errors, list valid values.
- For missing flags, show exact flag and type.
- Include use when it prevents another help call.
- Target under 50 tokens.
- For complex error payloads, use a TOON body after err instead of hint/use lines.

Example:
err missing_flag flag=--type
hint --type enum(decision|fact|pattern|observation)
use mem node add <text> --type TYPE

## --agent-out output format

### Status envelope (always AHF)

Success:
ok <kind> [count=<n>] [more=<0|1>] [cursor=<val>] [shown=<n>]

Error:
err <code> [key=value...]

Warning (non-fatal, after ok):
warn <code> [key=value...]

### Result body (TOON)

After the ok line, emit a TOON block. See https://github.com/toon-format/spec for full TOON syntax.

TOON format rules (from toon-go reference implementation):
- Length marker uses [#N] syntax, not [N].
- Rows are two-space indented.
- Values are quoted only when they contain commas or spaces.
- Go library: github.com/toon-format/toon-go

List example:
ok nodes count=3 more=0
nodes[#3]{id,type,tags,text}:
  n_102,fact,"project:billing|db",postgres 15 required
  n_088,decision,project:billing,migrate read model
  n_061,pattern,db|ops,use connection pool max 20
next inspect "mem search similar n_102 --agent-out"

Paginated list example:
ok builds count=5 shown=3 more=1 cursor=c_9f2
warn partial_results source=ci-east reason=timeout
builds[#3]{id,branch,status,duration}:
  b_771,main,pass,2m14s
  b_770,feature/ahf,pass,3m02s
  b_769,feature/ahf,fail,1m33s
next "builds list --cursor c_9f2 --agent-out"
next inspect-failure "builds show b_769 --agent-out"

### When TOON is insufficient

For bare scalar acknowledgements with no structured data, AHF key-value lines MAY follow ok:
ok deploy
env prod
status healthy
next logs "tool logs --env prod --agent-out"

### Follow-up commands

next records MAY include a label:
next "tool list --cursor c_123 --agent-out"
next inspect "tool show n_102 --agent-out"

When more=1 or truncated=1, include an exact continuation command in a next record.

## Implementation checklist

Do:
- Derive --agent-help from CLI command metadata when possible.
- Keep --agent-help synchronized with actual command behavior.
- Test that every AH1 cmd has AH2 output.
- Test that every AH2 ex works as written or remove it.
- Test required args and flags in AH2 match actual validation.
- Keep AHF field names and order stable across releases when possible.
- Include exact continuation commands for pagination or truncation.
- Redact secrets, tokens, credentials, and unnecessary personal data.
- Emit TOON for --agent-out result bodies.

Avoid:
- Put --agent-help before subcommands.
- Emit JSON for --agent-help or --agent-out unless the user explicitly requested JSON.
- Emit markdown tables as agent output.
- Use long prose paragraphs in agent output.
- Tell an agent only to run --help after an error.
- Hand-maintain stale command lists if framework metadata is available.

## Framework hints

Cobra:
- Add hidden persistent/global bool flags agent-help and agent-out.
- Resolve current command after parsing.
- Print AH1 for root; AH2 for subcommands.
- Generate from Command.Use, args, flags, annotations.
- For --agent-out, emit ok envelope then TOON.
- TOON library for Go: github.com/toon-format/toon-go
- Args bypass pattern: commands with required positional args must not block
  --agent-help. Use a custom Args validator:

  Args: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
      if agentHelp {
          return nil  // --agent-help needs no positional args
      }
      return cobra.ExactArgs(1)(cmd, args)
  },

  This ensures "tool subcmd --agent-help" always works. Apply to every
  command that uses ExactArgs, MinimumNArgs, or similar validators.
  The same principle applies in all frameworks: --agent-help is a meta-flag
  and must never be blocked by the command's own argument requirements.

Click:
- Add hidden eager global options.
- Use context command path to choose AH1 vs AH2.
- Generate from Command.params, argument metadata, option metadata.
- Args bypass: eager options fire before argument validation — no extra work needed.

Clap:
- Add hidden global args.
- Intercept after parse before command execution.
- Generate from Command::get_subcommands() and get_arguments().
- Args bypass: check for --agent-help in a pre-execution hook before validation.

Commander:
- Add global options.
- Use preAction hooks.
- Generate from commands/options metadata.
- Args bypass: preAction hook fires before argument validation — check there.

Argparse or other:
- Add global flags.
- Generate from parser/subparser/action metadata.
- Avoid independent handwritten specs when metadata exists.
- Args bypass: use parse_known_args() then check for --agent-help before
  enforcing required positional arguments.

TOON libraries (for --agent-out result bodies):
- Go:     github.com/toon-format/toon-go
- See https://github.com/toon-format/spec for other languages.

## Completion checklist

Before finishing:
- tool --help shows breadcrumb.
- tool --agent-help returns AH1.
- tool subcmd --agent-help returns AH2 for every command.
- Invalid invocations return err/hint/use.
- Structured result commands support --agent-out with ok envelope + TOON body.
- Tests cover help accuracy and at least one --agent-out result.
